$schema_comment: Sample config for an Anthropic-cloud /schedule runner (or any cron-driven runner) calling teammate agent routines. The runner reads this file, picks routines whose 'cron' fires now, runs `teammate agent run <name>`, and distributes the resulting markdown files to Slack / GitHub / wherever via its own scoped tokens.

routines.name: weekly_digest
routines.description: Monday 09:00 UTC. Posts the Slack chunk to #team-brain.

routines.name: orphan_triage
routines.description: Nightly 03:00 UTC. Stages one issue body per orphan; the runner opens / updates the issues.

routines.name: pr_migration_plan
routines.description: Fires on PR open / synchronize. The runner provides pr_number + pr_files.

notes: The agent itself never holds a token. The runner reads each artifact file the agent stages, then makes the API calls itself with its own scoped credentials.
If the agent fails (process error, brain unreachable), the runner reports the failure to the team channel without taking a side effect.
Routines are read-only on the brain. They never run `git commit` or `git push`.
